title: Beyond the inflection point: The new forces shaping the transformation of work
summary: Ageing populations, deglobalization and AI are reshaping work all at once — and employers expect 39% of core skills to change by 2030, making constant reskilling the new normal.

title: The AI-driven workforce is here. How should your industry transform?
summary: AI is projected to displace 92 million jobs but create 170 million new ones by 2030 — a net gain of 78 million — with the winning model being 'AI for execution, humans for judgment and creativity'.

title: How AI skills and experience are transforming the workplace
summary: New data shows workers who build real AI skills are seeing better wages and higher-quality jobs — proof that learning to use AI well is becoming a genuine career advantage.

title: How AI is changing the nature of entry-level work
summary: US entry-level job postings dropped 35% in 18 months as AI takes over routine tasks — and the work that remains looks different, with new hires joining client work sooner.

title: OECD Skills Outlook 2025
summary: A major OECD report on building the 21st-century skills people need to thrive, showing how things outside your control — like where you grow up — still shape who gets ahead.

title: Making AI Work: Why Investing in Skills Matters
summary: The OECD argues that whether AI actually pays off depends less on the tech and more on whether people have the skills to use it — making training the real game-changer.

title: Education Policy Outlook 2025
summary: Subtitled 'Nurturing Engaged and Resilient Lifelong Learners in a World of Digital Transformation', this OECD report looks at how education can keep up as digital tech and AI change how we learn.

title: Gen Zs and millennials are redefining career progression on their own terms
summary: Three-quarters of Gen Z now use AI daily at work — many to find learning opportunities and career advice — but they say they're adapting faster than their bosses and organizations can keep up.

title: 2026 Gen Z and Millennial Survey
summary: Deloitte's global survey finds Gen Z prioritising stability, well-being and meaning over fast promotions, while nearly 74% now use AI in daily work and see it as a tool for growth.

title: Talent Reinventors: Delivering value with and for people in the age of AI
summary: Companies that pair AI adoption with a people-first mindset are pulling ahead — and the skills that win are about teaming, mobility and learning on the job alongside AI.

title: Pulse of Change
summary: Bosses are confident about AI but workers aren't on board: only about 1 in 5 employees feel like active co-creators of how AI changes their job, making people — not tech — the biggest barrier.

title: Learning, Reinvented: Accelerating Human–AI Collaboration
summary: 84% of leaders expect people and AI to work side by side within three years, but only 11% of companies actually let humans and AI learn from each other — and those that do see 4x faster skill-building.

title: Agents of Change: On the 10th Anniversary of World Youth Skills Day
summary: UN leaders marked 10 years of World Youth Skills Day by pushing for every young person to get real digital and AI skills, warning that 62% of youth use AI but only 30% have had any training.

title: Listening to 200 young people about work, purpose and the future
summary: UNDP ran a Future of Work Academy where 200+ young people swapped notes on AI, green jobs and building meaningful careers — and the big takeaway was that curiosity beats content.

title: Four Young Creators Transforming Local Ideas Into Global Solutions Using Intellectual Property
summary: Four under-35 innovators from Angola, Brazil, the US and Vietnam show how understanding intellectual property turned their local ideas into real businesses solving global problems.

title: What 'mental health in all policies' can look like on the ground
summary: WHO spotlights France's Working First and Housing First programmes as proof that having a job and a home can actively support mental health recovery, not just follow it.

title: Skills Training Is Opening Opportunities for Millions of Young People in Africa
summary: World Bank-backed skills programmes across Africa are connecting millions of young people to real jobs, with new regional projects aiming to reach 28 million youth in 22 countries.

title: Partnering to Unlock Youth Employment: Q&A with Dr. Mona Mourshed
summary: The CEO of a nonprofit that has trained people into new careers across 17 countries explains how tying training to what employers actually want has helped graduates earn $2 billion in wages.

title: AI is Coming for Your Job. Now What?
summary: Robinhood's Vlad Tenev gives a clear-eyed take on what happens when AI reshapes most of today's jobs — and why the outcome might not be the doom you'd expect.

title: How to build a career you actually love
summary: Investor Bill Gurley argues that fascination beats 'follow your passion,' and that obsessive, lifelong learning is the real engine of a career that lights you up.

title: Is Your Job Safe from AI? A Career Consultant + a YouTuber Answer
summary: A workplace expert and a creator team up to break down how to thrive through career chaos and stay employable as AI changes the rules.

title: I'll Probably Lose My Job to AI. Here's Why That's OK
summary: Journalist Megan J. McArdle reframes AI job loss through the story of the 19th-century Luddites, asking what we'd really give up by halting progress to protect today's jobs.

title: 3 Possible Futures for AI — Which Will We Choose?
summary: After decades working in AI across the US and China, Alvin W. Graylin lays out three very different futures we could be heading toward — and why the choice is ours.

title: Top of the Charts: our most popular work in 2025
summary: Our World in Data's roundup of its most popular charts of the year — a fast tour of the biggest global trends in health, energy, economy and AI, all in one place.

title: Charted: The World's Fastest Growing Jobs (2025-2030)
summary: Almost every one of the world's fastest-growing jobs is tied to AI or robotics — think Big Data Specialists, FinTech Engineers and AI/ML roles leading the pack to 2030.

title: Mapped: AI Adoption by Country in 2026
summary: The UAE (70%) and Singapore (63%) lead the world in everyday AI use, while the US trails 20+ countries — a global map of who's actually using AI in 2026.

title: How People Are Actually Using AI at Work in 2026
summary: AI at work has grown up: decision-making is now the #1 use case (28%), as people lean on it for analysis and judgment rather than just writing emails.

title: Renewables overtook coal in the global electricity mix for the first time in a century
summary: Big milestone for the green economy: in 2025 renewables hit 33.8% of global electricity, edging past coal for the first time since 1919 thanks to a solar and wind boom.
